headline: Why is Donald Trump so mean?
description: By shamelessly flouting democratic norms and flaunting his amorality, brutality and bad faith, the American president restores pride to his humiliated electoral base, unsettles his opponents, and subverts the rules of the political game.

headline: US aviator rescued as Trump vows strikes on Iran's infrastructure
'Can Le Pen and Bardella accept betraying a portion of their own voters to get elected?'
'The power struggle between Saudi Arabia and the UAE is only on hold'
description: The current crisis has forced Riyadh and Abu Dhabi to set aside their disputes, which nevertheless persist, notably in Yemen, explains our columnist Jean-Pierre Filiu.

headline: European company sells planes to Myanmar junta despite sanctions
description: An investigation by Le Monde and the Centre for Information Resilience has uncovered the deliveries of four aircraft from a Czech company to the military dictatorship, despite an EU ban on the export of weapons and goods that could be used for military purposes.

headline: Looking for Cécile, a 'bad girl' in France's sinister former 'preservation schools'
description: Until the mid-20th century, correctional facilities run by the French prison administration held minors accused of theft or 'debauchery' in appalling conditions. Upon his retirement, Michel Bey discovered that his mother, Cécile, who died when he was 5, was one of them.

headline: Will Self: 'Our historical condition is a peculiar mixture of omniscience and impotence'
description: We are bombarded by a swarm of signals too numerous to interpret in time, and the world appears both predetermined and uncontrollable, observes the British writer in an op-ed for Le Monde.

'Literature and economics both claim to offer a representation of the world and possible scenarios'
description: The theme of debt recurs in many novels of the 19th century. Based on this observation, Alexandre Péraud, a literature professor, explains in an interview with Le Monde how the rise of the novel went hand in hand with the emergence of financial capitalism.

headline: What is Israel's military objective in Lebanon?
description: Israel continues to expand its operations, fueling fears of a new occupation of southern Lebanon. While the military's official mission is to completely disarm Hezbollah, its operational goal appears to be simply to weaken the militia-party and create a more or less extensive buffer zone along its border.

headline: Dinosaur egg trove discovered in southern France
description: The Dinosaur Museum in Mèze has announced the discovery of about 100 dinosaur eggs dating back approximately 72 million years, making this deposit one of the most significant in the world.
